SEARCH
Package com.example.cmput301_app.model

Class Poster

java.lang.Object
com.example.cmput301_app.model.Poster
public class Poster extends Object
Represents an event poster in the application. A poster is uploaded by an organizer and is associated with a specific event. It serves as a dedicated data object for managing event poster images separately from the Event class, allowing admins to browse and remove images independently of event management. When a poster is created or removed, PosterDB is responsible for keeping the posterUrl field on the corresponding Event document in Firestore in sync. Specifically: - When a poster is uploaded, PosterDB creates the Poster document and updates the posterUrl field on the associated Event document. - When a poster is removed, PosterDB deletes the Poster document, removes the image from Firebase Storage, and clears the posterUrl field on the associated Event document. Outstanding issues: - Image upload and deletion logic is handled entirely by PosterDB and Firebase Storage. This class models the poster data only.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Poster()
    Default no-argument constructor required for Firebase deserialization.
    Poster(String posterId, String eventId, String organizerId, String imageUrl, com.google.firebase.Timestamp uploadTimestamp)
    Constructs a Poster with all required fields.

  • Constructor Details

    • Poster

      public Poster()
      Default no-argument constructor required for Firebase deserialization.

    • Poster

      public Poster(String posterId, String eventId, String organizerId, String imageUrl, com.google.firebase.Timestamp uploadTimestamp)
      Constructs a Poster with all required fields.
      Parameters:
      posterId - the unique identifier for this poster
      eventId - the ID of the event this poster belongs to
      organizerId - the device ID of the organizer who uploaded this poster
      imageUrl - the Firebase Storage download URL for the poster image
      uploadTimestamp - the time this poster was uploaded

  • Method Details

    • getPosterId

      public String getPosterId()
      Returns the unique identifier for this poster.
      Returns:
      the poster ID string

    • setPosterId

      public void setPosterId(String posterId)
      Sets the unique identifier for this poster.
      Parameters:
      posterId - the poster ID to assign

    • getEventId

      public String getEventId()
      Returns the ID of the event this poster belongs to. Used by PosterDB to keep the posterUrl field on the Event document in sync.
      Returns:
      the event ID string

    • setEventId

      public void setEventId(String eventId)
      Sets the ID of the event this poster belongs to.
      Parameters:
      eventId - the event ID to assign

    • getOrganizerId

      public String getOrganizerId()
      Returns the device ID of the organizer who uploaded this poster.
      Returns:
      the organizer device ID string

    • setOrganizerId

      public void setOrganizerId(String organizerId)
      Sets the device ID of the organizer who uploaded this poster.
      Parameters:
      organizerId - the organizer device ID to assign

    • getImageUrl

      public String getImageUrl()
      Returns the Firebase Storage download URL for the poster image. This value is also stored on the associated Event document's posterUrl field, kept in sync by PosterDB.
      Returns:
      the image URL string, or null if not yet uploaded

    • setImageUrl

      public void setImageUrl(String imageUrl)
      Sets the Firebase Storage download URL for the poster image.
      Parameters:
      imageUrl - the image URL to assign

    • getUploadTimestamp

      public com.google.firebase.Timestamp getUploadTimestamp()
      Returns the timestamp when this poster was uploaded.
      Returns:
      the upload Timestamp

    • setUploadTimestamp

      public void setUploadTimestamp(com.google.firebase.Timestamp uploadTimestamp)
      Sets the timestamp when this poster was uploaded.
      Parameters:
      uploadTimestamp - the upload Timestamp to assign
